title: chrysanthemum vulgare
intro: Chrysanthemum vulgare, also known as the common chrysanthemum, is a type of flowering plant in the daisy family, characterized by its yellow flowers and bitter roots. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and diuretic effects, making it useful in treating fever, sore throats, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Chrysanthemum vulgare include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and boosting the immune system, while also acting as an antioxidant to protect against cell damage. In herbal preparations, Chrysanthemum vulgare is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being dried and used in soups and decoctions.
